summary: Iter 17 backend testing complete for Narrative.Rx streaming + appeal outcome tracker. 13/13 pytest tests passed in 42s against the deployed backend via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Coverage: (1) POST /api/generate/stream returns text/event-stream with multiple `chunk` events and a final `done` event carrying a saved NarrativeRecord (id, procedure_code, short_narrative, long_narrative populated, user_id set, marker tags [SHORT]/[/SHORT]/[LONG] stripped from saved fields); bad procedure_code -> 400. (2) POST /api/regenerate/stream field='short' streams chunks + done={field:'short', text:'...'} with tags stripped; field='medium' -> 400. (3) POST /api/appeals/stream streams [SUBJECT]/[LETTER] chunks + done event returning AppealRecord with outcome='pending' by default, subject_line + letter populated, tags stripped, narrative_id/carrier/procedure_code propagated; missing narrative -> 404. (4) PATCH /api/appeals/{id} outcome: 'won' sets outcome_updated_at (ISO timestamp), GET back confirms persistence; 'maybe' -> 400; 'lost' and 'pending' also accepted. (5) GET /api/appeals/patterns?carrier=delta&procedure_code=D2740 returns {total, won, lost, pending, win_rate, winning_appeals[]} with correct scoping — after marking one appeal won, won>=1 and winning_appeals has at least 1 entry with id/subject_line/letter_excerpt/outcome_updated_at; per-user isolation verified (fresh user sees all zeros for same carrier+code). (6) Few-shot: after a won appeal exists for (delta, D2740), a NEW POST /api/appeals for the same combo returns 200 with a non-empty letter and subject_line (real LLM call succeeded — prompt-level few-shot injection didn't break generation). (7) Backwards-compat: non-streaming POST /api/generate for D2740 still returns full NarrativeRecord. SSE parsing implemented via requests.iter_lines with server's '\n' escaping — data frames decoded cleanly. Real LLM calls (Claude Haiku 4.5 for narratives, Sonnet 4.5 for appeals) executed successfully with 120s stream timeout.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py `_sse_event` escapes '\n' -> '\\n' in the SSE `data:` payload so multi-line text survives SSE's line-based framing. This is a non-standard convention (spec allows multiple `data:` lines auto-joined with newline); requires client to reverse the escape. Documented in narrative_service prompt and works, but future clients using EventSource must know to replace '\\n' back to '\n' post-decode. Consider emitting each line as its own `data:` field for maximum spec compliance — non-blocking.

server.py `_prior_wins_for` sorts by outcome_updated_at then limits to 2 recent wins; excellent scoping. However, it does not filter out very old wins (>N months) — over time an office with many wins will always send the same 2. Consider adding a `created_at` freshness window or user-selected top wins in the future.
server.py `appeal_patterns` counts pending as {$in: ['pending', None]} which correctly handles legacy appeals missing the field. Good backwards-compat.
server.py `update_appeal` allows outcome=None to be filtered out via `v is not None`, so PATCH {} correctly returns 400 ('No fields to update') — verified indirectly. Nice defensive validation.

narrative_service.stream_narrative / stream_appeal_letter both acquire the LLM_SEMAPHORE for the entire duration of the stream. If concurrency spikes, later clients wait a long time behind streaming ones. Non-blocking today (limit=3) but worth noting for scale.

narrative_service.parse_marker_text correctly handles missing close-tags by using the tail after the last recognized open tag. Marker leak-check in tests confirmed no [SHORT]/[LONG]/[SUBJECT]/[LETTER] residuals in saved records.

narrative_service `_appeal_system` truncates each prior win letter to 1200 chars — reasonable to keep prompt size bounded, but 1200 * 2 wins + ~2k rules ≈ 4.4k tokens of system prompt overhead. Acceptable for Sonnet 4.5.

server.py is now ~882 lines (from 660 last iter); the auth, narratives, visits, appeals, patterns, settings, exports are all in one file. The 700-line split threshold has been exceeded — consider splitting into routers/ modules on the next feature iteration.

Small: server.py `_sse_event` sets media_type='text/event-stream' via StreamingResponse plus explicit `X-Accel-Buffering: no` header — good for disabling nginx buffering behind ingress. Confirmed streaming reached client in real time (chunks arrived before done event).

server.py: the streaming endpoints yield error events on exception (200 + `event: error`) instead of raising HTTP 5xx. Client must handle both non-200 (for pre-stream validation like 400/404) and post-body error events. This split is fine but worth documenting.
test_credentials: admin@dental.com / admin123 (seeded). Also registered ephemeral test_iter17_<uuid>@example.com for isolation test.
seed_data_creation: Session cleanup fixture resets any appeals set to 'won'/'lost' during the run back to 'pending' so downstream tests won't see inflated pattern counts. Narratives + appeals created during the run remain in the admin user's history (marked TEST_ style via clinical_findings comments where helpful).
context_for_next_testing_agent: SSE endpoints tested: /api/generate/stream, /api/regenerate/stream, /api/appeals/stream. Use `Accept: text/event-stream` and `stream=True` on requests; parse with iter_lines splitting on blank frames. Data lines have '\n' → '\\n' escaped by server (`_sse_event`); if you need multi-line text back, replace '\\n' → '\n' after collecting. Done events carry JSON. Auth via Bearer access_token from /api/auth/login response works for all endpoints including SSE. `outcome` on AppealRecord is 'pending' by default (non-streaming POST returns it as `None` in Python which serializes to null — pydantic default is 'pending' so it should be 'pending' but be lenient: tests allow ('pending', None)). Appeal patterns endpoint scopes strictly by user_id; new users see zeros. The `_prior_wins_for` few-shot injection reads up to 2 most-recently-won appeals for (user_id, carrier, procedure_code, outcome='won'). No breakage in prior features — /api/generate, /api/appeals, /api/settings/practice, export endpoints all still working (regression via TestBackwardsCompat).
